name: Farmers National Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Northwest Illinois since 1902 with branches in Geneseo, Prophetstown, Morrison, and Mount Carroll. Specializes in agricultural lending and comprehensive financial services.
description_long:
Farmers National Bank has been operating as a locally owned and managed community bank in Northwest Illinois for over 120 years, establishing deep roots in the agricultural communities it serves. The bank maintains four physical locations across Carroll and Whiteside counties and has prioritized accessibility through modern digital banking platforms, including mobile apps and online account opening capabilities.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of financial services including agriculture loans (which represent approximately 75% of their loan portfolio), checking and savings accounts, digital banking, retirement planning, farm management services, IRAs, HSAs, and mortgage services. They provide deposit rate information, mortgage rate quotes, and maintain a routing number (071108423) for standard banking transactions. Their digital infrastructure supports online check deposit, remote deposit capture (RDC), and 24-hour mobile banking access.

Farmers National Bank distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on agricultural lending and community engagement. The bank ranks in the top 100 community banks nationally for agriculture lending annually. Leadership emphasizes that agriculture lending is "what we do" and "who we are," with staff trained specifically in agricultural finance. The bank actively participates in local community events including county fairs, 4-H programs, and school fundraising, demonstrating commitment beyond transactional banking.

As a regional community bank, Farmers National Bank is well-positioned for customers seeking personalized service with agricultural expertise, though customers requiring extensive branch networks or premium investment products may need to supplement services elsewhere. The bank's strength lies in understanding local agricultural cycles and farm-specific financial planning rather than national consumer banking convenience. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online check deposit and remote deposit capture (RDC)
Farm management services (corporate trustee, executor, absentee property representation)
pros: Top 100 national ranking for agricultural lending among community banks, with ~75% of portfolio in agriculture loans
Locally owned and managed for 120+ years with deep community roots and active involvement in county fairs, 4-H programs, and local schools
Staff trained in agricultural finance with demonstrated understanding of farming industry needs and cycles
Modern digital banking capabilities including mobile app, online account opening, online check deposit, and 24-hour RDC access
Comprehensive financial services including farm management, retirement planning, corporate trustee services, and absentee property management
Multiple branch locations across Northwest Illinois (Geneseo, Prophetstown, Morrison, Mount Carroll) serving Carroll and Whiteside counties
Competitive deposit rates and mortgage rate offerings with published rate information available on website
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Northwest Illinois only—not accessible to customers outside four-county service area
No evidence of national investment products or wealth management services beyond basic retirement planning and farm management
No information provided about loan rates, APR terms, or specific product pricing on website
Limited branch network (4 locations) compared to regional and national banks, reducing convenience for customers needing multiple locations
No mention of business banking services, merchant services, or commercial lending beyond agricultural loans
best_for: Agricultural operators and farmers seeking specialized ag lending with industry-knowledgeable staff in Northwest Illinois
Multi-generational farming families seeking farm management, estate planning, and trustee services in their community
Small business owners in rural Illinois communities wanting personalized banking with local decision-making
Customers prioritizing community engagement and local ownership over national banking convenience
diagnosis: Farmers National Bank is ideal for agricultural operators and farming families in Northwest Illinois seeking specialized ag lending from staff with deep industry expertise and community commitment. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to four Illinois counties—this bank is not accessible to customers outside their service area and lacks the branch network of larger regional institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
